creator: james.pye@gmail.com
text:
Apparently, IOCrest released a PCI-E 4.0 1x card that uses this chipset (AQC113) a couple months ago. Most other *full* 10Gbe options, to my knowledge, require 4x physical interfaces, which are becoming increasingly rare on modern consumer boards outside of M.2. (M.2) Interfaces and adapters to other options do exist, but cost you that m.2 slot and will obstruct a PCI slot or offer an alternative inconvenience.

If the port doesn't work, sounds like it does not, routing through a linux VM should.
